text: <par>According to Judson, Written Burmese <latinform>yam</latinform> is a loanword from Pali, meaning 'division; watch of the night' (cf. Lahu <latinform>yâ(n)</latinform> 'time' < Shan < Burmese), but the other Burmish forms, as well as those from Himalayish languages, suggest that this may actually reflect a PTB root.</par>
